data.title: Former PayPal exec José Fernández da Ponte becomes President of Stellar Development Foundation to boost global growth for XLM.
data.description: Former PayPal executive, José Fernández da Ponte, has taken on the roles of President and Chief Growth Officer at the Stellar (XLM) Development Foundation. His aim is to boost digital asset adoption and scale infrastructure for the project.

data.title: Novartis raises outlook, reports Q2 earnings of $14.05 billion with 12% sales growth and $5.93 billion operating income. Initiates $10 billion buyback.
data.description: Swiss pharmaceutical company Novartis AG (NYSE: NVS) released its second-quarter 2025 earnings report, announcing an increase in its full-year earnings forecast. Sales for the quarter amounted to $14.05 billion, just slightly below analysts' expectations of $14.06 billion. Year-on-year, net sales saw a 12% rise (+11% on constant currency basis) with core operating income reaching $5.93 billion - up by 20% (+21% on constant currency basis). Notable sales growth was attributed to the performance of key products including breast cancer treatment Kisqali ($1.18 billion, +64% cc), heart failure drug Entresto ($2.36 billion, +22% cc), multiple sclerosis treatment Kesimpta ($1.08 billion, +33% cc), Scemblix ($298 million, +79% cc), and Leqvio ($298 million). The corrected title now accurately states that the company raised its outlook, contrary to the initial misrepresentation of an "outlook cut". For the complete story, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Vegamour unveils GRO+ Advanced Supplements for hair longevity, a dual-pill system developed by MDs.
data.description: On July 17, 2025, in Los Angeles, Vegamour, a company specializing in hair longevity through plant-based biotechnology, revealed the release of their newest product: GRO+ Advanced Hair Growth & Density Supplements. This innovative dual-pill system, developed by medical doctors and supported by extensive clinical research, is set to revolutionize the hair growth industry.

data.title: Festi hf. expects EBITDA of ISK 3.9 billion in Q2 2025, up from ISK 2.9 billion last year, an increase of ISK 1.0 billion YoY.
data.description: The preliminary management accounts for Q2 2025 indicate that the EBITDA is projected to be ISK 3.9 billion, a significant rise from ISK 2.9 billion recorded in the corresponding period in the previous year. This marks a year-on-year increase of ISK 1.0 billion, or ISK 0.6 billion when excluding Lyfja EBITDA, as Lyfja was not under the Group during the same quarter last year.

data.title: Canary files for Staked INJ ETF as interest in altcoin funds rises. Investors can earn yield on underlying tokens. SEC filing Thursday.
data.description: On Thursday, the asset manager submitted a filing to the SEC regarding the introduction of its new Staked INJ ETF. This ETF will provide investors with the opportunity to generate yield on the underlying tokens of the fund.

data.title: Bipartisan majorities oppose US disengaging from UN agencies and sanctioning ICC for arrest warrants against US allied leaders - UMD survey.
data.description: President Trump recently issued executive orders to reconsider the United States' commitment to UN agencies, including withdrawing from the World Health Organization. This decision comes after the International Criminal Court (ICC) issued arrest warrants against American allied leaders. As a response, President Trump opposes sanctioning the ICC for their actions. The executive orders aim to reassess the US involvement in international organizations. The specific details of these orders involve evaluating the country's role in UN agencies and potentially imposing measures against them. President Trump's actions represent a shift in foreign policy direction concerning international collaborations and alliances, particularly in response to the ICC's actions.

data.title: Asia-Pacific leads in nonwoven filter media market due to industrial growth in China and India. Boston, July 17, 2025.
data.description: In the evolving landscape of the nonwoven filter media market, the Asia-Pacific region has emerged as a dominant force. This shift is driven by the rapid industrialization, urban expansion, and flourishing manufacturing sectors in China and India. The latest research from BCC reveals that this market trend is notable and significant. The study, conducted in July 2025 in Boston, highlights the growing influence of the Asia-Pacific region in this sector. The study emphasizes the vast potential in these markets, showcasing key numbers and indicators that underscore the region's position as a powerhouse in the nonwoven filter media market.

data.title: Ben & Jerry’s will cool down Torontonians on National Ice Cream Day with free ice cream from Doughlivery truck.
data.description: In celebration of National Ice Cream Day this summer in Toronto on July 17, 2025, Ben & Jerry’s will be launching the Doughlivery by Ben & Jerry’s truck campaign. The renowned ice cream brand will be hitting the streets to provide a cool escape from the soaring temperatures. The event will feature opportunities for free ice cream and additional surprises for Ben & Jerry’s supporters in Toronto. This activation aims to assist fans in beating the heat while indulging in some sweet treats.

data.title: Investigation launched by Purcell & Lefkowitz LLP into Vertiv Holdings Co (NYSE: VRT) for possible fiduciary duty breaches.
data.description: Purcell & Lefkowitz LLP disclosed on July 17, 2025, that they are conducting an investigation into Vertiv Holdings Co (NYSE: VRT) on behalf of shareholders. The inquiry aims to assess whether directors at Vertiv Holdings Co may have violated their fiduciary obligations towards the company.

data.title: Rexel plans to buy back shares twice a year starting June 30, 2025. #ShareBuyback #Rexel #June302025
data.description: A recent report indicates that there has been a significant increase in cyber attacks globally, with a total of 4,000 attacks reported in the last month alone. These attacks have targeted various industries, resulting in an estimated loss of over $100 million. The rise in cyber attacks has raised concerns among experts, who are urging companies to enhance their security measures to protect sensitive data. It is evident that the frequency and sophistication of these attacks are on the rise, posing a major threat to businesses and organizations worldwide. In response to this growing threat, it is crucial for companies to invest in robust cybersecurity measures to safeguard their digital assets and prevent financial losses.

data.title: Rexel to launch share buyback program by end of 2024.
data.description: A new report reveals that the global economy is projected to grow by 6% in 2021, the fastest rate recorded since 1973. This is a significant increase from the 3.2% contraction experienced in 2020 due to the COVID-19 pandemic. Emerging and developing economies are expected to grow by 6.7%, with China leading the pack at 8.4% growth. Advanced economies are predicted to grow by 5.6%, with the United States expected to see a growth rate of 6.9%, the highest in over 40 years. However, the report also warns of potential long-term scarring effects from the pandemic, particularly in countries with limited access to vaccines.

data.title: Sarepta Therapeutics' stock rises after FDA requests black box warning for Elevidys gene therapy. Expert panel reviewing liver injury cases and proposing enhanced immunosuppressive regimen for non-ambulant patients. Company halts Elevidys shipments temporarily in June. Will submit findings to FDA for approval.
data.description: Sarepta Therapeutics' stock (NASDAQ: SRPT) rose after an after-hours update on label expansion efforts for Elevidys, its gene therapy for Duchenne muscular dystrophy. FDA requested a black box warning for acute liver injury and failure, which Sarepta agrees with, resolving concerns for ambulant patients. Shipments for non-ambulant patients were temporarily halted in June, and a committee is reviewing cases and considering enhanced immunosuppressive regimens. The company will submit findings and proposed protocol to the FDA for discussion and assess real-world data opportunities for ambulant patients. This update is detailed on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Landsbankinn reported ISK 18.3 billion profit in H1 2025, with an ROE of 11.5%. Net interest income was ISK 32.5 billion. Net fee/commission income was ISK 6.2 billion. TM's insurance performance yielded ISK 925 million. Total capital ratio stood at 24.0%. Bank finalized AT1 issuance at USD 100 million, and issued senior bonds totaling NOK 500 million and SEK 1,300 million. AGM approved dividend payment.
data.description: Landsbankinn reported a profit of ISK 18.3 billion after tax in the first half of 2025, with ISK 10.4 billion in the second quarter. Their annualized ROE was 11.5%, up from 10.5% the previous year. Net interest margin on average total assets was 2.9%, and for domestic households, it was 2.1%. Net interest income was ISK 32.5 billion, while net fee and commission income totaled ISK 6.2 billion. TM's performance in the same period from insurance contracts was ISK 925 million, with a combined ratio of 93.2%. The cost-income ratio was 35.8%, and the total capital ratio stood at 24.0%. Landsbankinn issued AT1 securities worth USD 100 million and senior non-preferred bonds totaling NOK 500 million and SEK 1,300 million, with high demand. The FSA requires a total capital ratio of 20.4%. The Bank's AGM approved a dividend payment.

data.title: Purcell & Lefkowitz LLP investigates Wyndham Hotels & Resorts, Inc. (NYSE: WH) on behalf of shareholders.
data.description: A law firm in New York, Purcell & Lefkowitz LLP, has disclosed that it is currently conducting an investigation into Wyndham Hotels & Resorts, Inc. (NYSE: WH) in representation of the company's shareholders. The investigation is centered on examining whether the directors of Wyndham Hotels & Resorts may have violated their fiduciary responsibilities.
